title: Clutia vaccinioides
taxonomy: Clutia vaccinioides is classified as a species within the family Phyllanthaceae. This family is part of the larger order Malpighiales and is commonly known as the Phyllanthaceae or five-finger family. The class to which Clutia vaccinioides belongs is Magnoliopsida, which is one of the two main classes of flowering plants. The clade that includes Clutia vaccinioides is the Angiosperms, which are the flowering plants that are characterized by the production of seeds within a flower. Clutia vaccinioides's classification is further detailed with the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Clutia vaccinioides is known for its medicinal properties in traditional medicine. In some regions, the plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The leaves and roots of Clutia vaccinioides are often used in herbal remedies due to their supposed antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these medicinal uses. Traditional healers continue to rely on Clutia vaccinioides for its perceived health benefits.

intro: Clutia vaccinioides, also known as Vaccinio-Clutia, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Clutia vaccinioides include flavonoids, tannins, and other phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Clutia vaccinioides is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical applications and ointments to leverage its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory properties.
